content:
# Afm

**Summary**: A technique to image surface morphology and topography at the nanoscale.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-kasha|Kasha, 2014]] used AFM to visualize surface changes after contact with fluids.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] used AFM scans to confirm the exact particle size of the biosynthesized AgNP.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] used AFM scans to confirm the exact particle size of the biosynthesized AgNP.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Used to observe surface roughness on MEC samples which were too rough for high-resolution imaging. ([[2014-kasha|Kasha, 2014, p. 72]])

- The characteristic size of these particles was confirmed as having a polyhedral structure with a base width of about 30 nm and height of about 1.4 nm. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 138]])
